content:
2 min read
Just now

--

I work full time managing enterprise infrastructure. I have no dedicated study time built into my workday. I have evenings and weekends.
I passed AWS Solutions Architect Associate in 6 weeks.
Not because I’m exceptional at exams. Because I had a system. Here it is, exactly as I ran it.

Week 1–2: Understand the map, not the details
The biggest mistake people make studying for AWS certifications is going deep on services before they understand how services relate to each other.
Spend the first two weeks on architecture, not features. Understand VPC before you learn every EC2 instance type. Understand IAM before you memorise every S3 storage class.
The exam tests whether you can design solutions. You can’t design what you don’t understand structurally.
Resource: AWS’s own free “AWS Cloud Practitioner Essentials” course. Even if you’re going for Solutions Architect, start here. Takes 6 hours. Worth every minute.

Week 3–4: Hands-on every service you read about
Reading about S3 lifecycle policies is not the same as configuring one.
I have a personal AWS account. Free tier. Every service I studied, I built something with it. Small. Doesn’t matter. The act of configuring it makes it real in a way that reading never does.
The exam will describe a scenario and ask which service solves it. If you’ve actually configured the service, the answer is usually obvious. If you’ve only read about it, you’re guessing between two plausible options.

Week 5: Practice exams only
Tutorials Dojo practice exams. Not free but worth the ₹1,500. 6 full practice tests with detailed explanations for every answer including the wrong ones.
Understanding why wrong answers are wrong is more valuable than memorising right answers. The exam writes distractors that are almost right. Learning to spot the difference is the skill.
Target score on practice exams: 80%+ consistently before booking the real exam.

Week 6: Review weak areas only
Look at your practice exam results. Find the domains where you’re scoring below 70%. Study only those. Don’t review what you already know — you’re wasting revision time.
For me it was databases. I knew EC2 and S3 well from work. RDS, Aurora, DynamoDB — I’d touched them less. Week 6 was databases, databases, databases.

The exam itself
65 questions. 130 minutes. More time than you think.
Read every question twice. AWS exam questions are precisely worded and the difference between the right answer and the second-best answer is often one word — “most cost-effective” vs “highest availability” vs “least operational overhead.”
Those three phrases mean completely different things and point to completely different services.

Total investment
6 weeks × 1.5 hours per weekday evening + 3 hours each weekend day = roughly 65 hours of study.
Exam fee: approximately ₹12,000.
Practice exams: ₹1,500.
Salary increase from having the certification: significantly more than ₹13,500.
The maths is straightforward.
— Prasanta
